key_items: Bitcoin ETFs record 6-day loss streak with $1.55B net outflows, pushing 2026 toward net negative inflows
Bitcoin dives below $75K for first time in a month amid $1B in crypto liquidations and weak demand metrics
Fed minutes signal rate-hike risk rather than cuts, undermining Bitcoin's macro tailwind narrative
reasoning: Near-term technical breakdown below $75K combined with deteriorating ETF flows and hawkish Fed signals create a bearish swing setup. Positive long-term catalysts (AI integration, corporate accumulation, regulatory progress) are insufficient to offset immediate selling pressure and demand weakness.
risks: Liquidation cascade below $72.5K support could trigger panic selling and force further downside toward $70K if macro sentiment shifts negative or additional large holders exit positions.
